Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.seek_func = va_arg(param, curl_seek_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SEEKDATA:\n    /*\n     * Seek control callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.seek_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IOCTLFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * I/O control callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.ioctl_func = va_arg(param, curl_ioctl_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IOCTLDATA:\n    /*\n     * I/O control data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.ioctl_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERT:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CERT],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CERT_PROXY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERTTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT_TYPE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERTTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT_TYPE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEY:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEY_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_KEY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_KEY_PROXY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_TYPE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_TYPE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_KEYPASSWD:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL or SSH private key password.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_PASSWD],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_KEYPASSWD:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL private key password for proxy.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_PASSWD_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLENGINE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL crypto engine.\n     */\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr && argptr[0]) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ENGINE], argptr);\n      if(!result) {\n        result = Curl_ssl_set_engine(data, argptr);\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSLENGINE_DEFAULT:\n    /*\n     * flag to set engine as default.\n     */\n    Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ENGINE], NULL);\n    result = Curl_ssl_set_engine_default(data);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CRLF:\n    /*\n     * Kludgy option to enable CRLF conversions. Subject for removal.\n     */\n    data->set.crlf = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_HAPROXYPROTOCOL:\n    /*\n     * Set to send the HAProxy Proxy Protocol header\n     */\n    data->set.haproxyprotocol = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP:\n    /*\n     * Set the client IP to send through HAProxy PROXY protocol\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    /* We enable implicitly the HAProxy protocol if we use this flag. */\n    data->set.haproxyprotocol = TRUE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_INTERFACE:\n    /*\n     * Set what interface or address/hostname to bind the socket to when\n     * performing an operation and thus what from-IP your connection will use.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DEVICE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_BINDLOCAL\n  case CURLOPT_LOCALPORT:\n    /*\n     * Set what local port to bind the socket to when performing an operation.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.localport = curlx_sltous(arg);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_LOCALPORTRANGE:\n    /*\n     * Set number of local ports to try, starting with CURLOPT_LOCALPORT.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.localportrange = curlx_sltous(arg);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_GSSAPI_DELEGATION:\n    /*\n     * GSS-API credential delegation bitmask\n     */\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    data->set.gssapi_delegation = (unsigned char)uarg&\n      (CURLGSSAPI_DELEGATION_POLICY_FLAG|CURLGSSAPI_DELEGATION_FLAG);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying.\n     */\n    data->set.ssl.primary.verifypeer = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->ssl_config.verifypeer =\n        data->set.ssl.primary.verifypeer;\n    }\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying for DoH.\n     */\n    data->set.doh_verifypeer = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying for proxy.\n     */\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifypeer =\n      (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n\n    /* Update the current connection proxy_ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->proxy_ssl_config.verifypeer =\n        data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifypeer;\n    }\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the host name in the peer certificate\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    /* Obviously people are not reading documentation and too many thought\n       this argument took a boolean when it wasn't and misused it.\n       Treat 1 and 2 the same */\n    data->set.ssl.primary.verifyhost = (bool)((arg & 3) ? TRUE : FALSE);\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->ssl_config.verifyhost =\n        data->set.ssl.primary.verifyhost;\n    }\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the host name in the peer certificate for DoH\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    /* Treat both 1 and 2 as TRUE */\n    data->set.doh_verifyhost = (bool)((arg & 3) ? TRUE : FALSE);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the host name in the peer certificate for proxy\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    /* Treat both 1 and 2 as TRUE */\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifyhost = (bool)((arg & 3)?TRUE:FALSE);\n\n    /* Update the current connection proxy_ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->proxy_ssl_config.verifyhost =\n        data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifyhost;\n    }\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYSTATUS:\n    /*\n     * Enable certificate status verifying.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_cert_status_request()) {\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    data->set.ssl.primary.verifystatus = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->ssl_config.verifystatus =\n        data->set.ssl.primary.verifystatus;\n    }\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYSTATUS:\n    /*\n     * Enable certificate status verifying for DoH.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_cert_status_request()) {\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    data->set.doh_verifystatus = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CTX_FUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set a SSL_CTX callback\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX))\n      data->set.ssl.fsslctx = va_arg(param, curl_ssl_ctx_callback);\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CTX_DATA:\n    /*\n     * Set a SSL_CTX callback parameter pointer\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX))\n      data->set.ssl.fsslctxp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_FALSESTART:\n    /*\n     * Enable TLS false start.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_false_start(data)) {\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    data->set.ssl.falsestart = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CERTINFO:\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CERTINFO))\n      data->set.ssl.certinfo = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify file name of the public key in DER format.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY))\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_PINNEDPUBLICKEY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify file name of the public key in DER format.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY))\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_PINNEDPUBLICKEY_PROXY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CAINFO:\n    /*\n     * Set CA info for SSL connection. Specify file name of the CA certificate\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CAINFO_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection.\n     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB))\n      result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CAINFO],\n                               va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAINFO:\n    /*\n     * Set CA info SSL connection for proxy. Specify file name of the\n     * CA certificate\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAINFO_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection proxy.\n     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB))\n      result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CAINFO_PROXY],\n                               va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CAPATH:\n    /*\n     * Set CA path info for SSL connection. Specify directory name of the CA\n     * certificates which have been prepared using openssl c_rehash utility.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_PATH))\n      /* This does not work on windows. */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAPATH:\n    /*\n     * Set CA path info for SSL connection proxy. Specify directory name of the\n     * CA certificates which have been prepared using openssl c_rehash utility.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_PATH))\n      /* This does not work on windows. */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH_PROXY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CRLFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set CRL file info for SSL connection. Specify file name of the CRL\n     * to check certificates revocation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CRLFILE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CRLFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set CRL file info for SSL connection for proxy. Specify file name of the\n     * CRL to check certificates revocation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CRLFILE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT:\n    /*\n     * Set Issuer certificate file\n     * to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ISSUERCERT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds Issuer certificate to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_ISSUERCERT:\n    /*\n     * Set Issuer certificate file\n     * to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds Issuer certificate to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TELNET\n  case CURLOPT_TELNETOPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * Set a linked list of telnet options\n     */\n    data->set.telnet_options = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_BUFFERSIZE:\n    /*\n     * The application kindly asks for a differently sized receive buffer.\n     * If it seems reasonable, we'll use it.\n     */\n    if(data->state.buffer)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    if(arg > READBUFFER_MAX)\n      arg = READBUFFER_MAX;\n    else if(arg < 1)\n      arg = READBUFFER_SIZE;\n    else if(arg < READBUFFER_MIN)\n      arg = READBUFFER_MIN;\n\n    data->set.buffer_size = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD_BUFFERSIZE:\n    /*\n     * The application kindly asks for a differently sized upload buffer.\n     * Cap it to sensible.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    if(arg > UPLOADBUFFER_MAX)\n      arg = UPLOADBUFFER_MAX;\n    else if(arg < UPLOADBUFFER_MIN)\n      arg = UPLOADBUFFER_MIN;\n\n    data->set.upload_buffer_size = (unsigned int)arg;\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf); /* force a realloc next opportunity */\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_NOSIGNAL:\n    /*\n     * The application asks not to set any signal() or alarm() handlers,\n     * even when using a timeout.\n     */\n    data->set.no_signal = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SHARE:\n  {\n    struct Curl_share *set;\n    set = va_arg(param, struct Curl_share *);\n\n    /* disconnect from old share, if any */\n    if(data->share) {\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n      if(data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_SHARED) {\n        data->dns.hostcache = NULL;\n        data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_NONE;\n      }\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n      if(data->share->cookies == data->cookies)\n        data->cookies = NULL;\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n      if(data->share->hsts == data->hsts)\n        data->hsts = NULL;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n      if(data->share->sslsession == data->state.session)\n        data->state.session = NULL;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n      if(data->psl == &data->share->psl)\n        data->psl = data->multi? &data->multi->psl: NULL;\n#endif\n\n      data->share->dirty--;\n\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n      data->share = NULL;\n    }\n\n    if(GOOD_SHARE_HANDLE(set))\n      /* use new share if it set */\n      data->share = set;\n    if(data->share) {\n\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n      data->share->dirty++;\n\n      if(data->share->specifier & (1<< CURL_LOCK_DATA_DNS)) {\n        /* use shared host cache */\n        data->dns.hostcache = &data->share->hostcache;\n        data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_SHARED;\n      }\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n      if(data->share->cookies) {\n        /* use shared cookie list, first free own one if any */\n        Curl_cookie_cleanup(data->cookies);\n        /* enable cookies since we now use a share that uses cookies! */\n        data->cookies = data->share->cookies;\n      }\n#endif   /* CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n      if(data->share->hsts) {\n        /* first free the private one if any */\n        Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n        data->hsts = data->share->hsts;\n      }\n#endif   /* CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n      if(data->share->sslsession) {\n        data->set.general_ssl.max_ssl_sessions = data->share->max_ssl_sessions;\n        data->state.session = data->share->sslsession;\n      }\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n      if(data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL))\n        data->psl = &data->share->psl;\n#endif\n\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n    }\n    /* check for host cache not needed,\n     * it will be done by curl_easy_perform */\n  }\n  break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PRIVATE:\n    /*\n     * Set private data pointer.\n     */\n    data->set.private_data = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE:\n    /*\n     * Set the maximum size of a file to download.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.max_filesize = arg;\n    break;\n\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  case CURLOPT_USE_SSL:\n    /*\n     * Make transfers attempt to use SSL/TLS.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURLUSESSL_NONE) || (arg >= CURLUSESSL_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.use_ssl = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    data->set.ssl.primary.ssl_options = (unsigned char)(arg & 0xff);\n    data->set.ssl.enable_beast = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_ALLOW_BEAST);\n    data->set.ssl.no_revoke = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_REVOKE);\n    data->set.ssl.no_partialchain = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_PARTIALCHAIN);\n    data->set.ssl.revoke_best_effort = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_REVOKE_BEST_EFFORT);\n    data->set.ssl.native_ca_store = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NATIVE_CA);\n    data->set.ssl.auto_client_cert = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_AUTO_CLIENT_CERT);\n    /* If a setting is added here it should also be added in dohprobe()\n       which sets its own CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS based on these settings. */\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_OPTIONS:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.ssl_options = (unsigned char)(arg & 0xff);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.enable_beast = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_ALLOW_BEAST);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.no_revoke = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_REVOKE);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.no_partialchain = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_PARTIALCHAIN);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.revoke_best_effort =\n      !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_REVOKE_BEST_EFFORT);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.native_ca_store = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NATIVE_CA);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.auto_client_cert =\n      !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_AUTO_CLIENT_CERT);\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_EC_CURVES:\n    /*\n     * Set accepted curves in SSL connection setup.\n     * Specify colon-delimited list of curve algorithm names.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_EC_CURVES],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_IPRESOLVE:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURL_IPRESOLVE_WHATEVER) || (arg > CURL_IPRESOLVE_V6))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.ipver = (unsigned char) arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * Set the maximum size of a file to download.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.max_filesize = bigsize;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_NODELAY:\n    /*\n     * Enable or disable TCP_NODELAY, which will disable/enable the Nagle\n     * algorithm\n     */\n    data->set.tcp_nodelay = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_IGNORE_CONTENT_LENGTH:\n    data->set.ignorecl = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_ONLY:\n    /*\n     * No data transfer.\n     * (1) - only do connection\n     * (2) - do first get request but get no content\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg > 2)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.connect_only = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKOPTFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * socket callback function: called after socket() but before connect()\n     */\n    data->set.fsockopt = va_arg(param, curl_sockopt_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKOPTDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.sockopt_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_OPENSOCKETFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * open/create socket callback function: called instead of socket(),\n     * before connect()\n     */\n    data->set.fopensocket = va_arg(param, curl_opensocket_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_OPENSOCKETDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.opensocket_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CLOSESOCKETFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * close socket callback function: called instead of close()\n     * when shutting down a connection\n     */\n    data->set.fclosesocket = va_arg(param, curl_closesocket_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVER_START_FUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * resolver start callback function: called before a new resolver request\n     * is started\n     */\n    data->set.resolver_start = va_arg(param, curl_resolver_start_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVER_START_DATA:\n    /*\n     * resolver start callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.resolver_start_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CLOSESOCKETDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.closesocket_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_SESSIONID_CACHE:\n    data->set.ssl.primary.sessionid = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.sessionid = data->set.ssl.primary.sessionid;\n#endif\n    break;\n\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n    /* we only include SSH options if explicitly built to support SSH */\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_AUTH_TYPES:\n    data->set.ssh_auth_types = (unsigned int)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_PUBLIC_KEYFILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.ssh/id_dsa.pub file\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_PRIVATE_KEYFILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.ssh/id_dsa file\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5:\n    /*\n     * Option to allow for the MD5 of the host public key to be checked\n     * for validation purposes.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS:\n    /*\n     * Store the file name to read known hosts from.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256:\n    /*\n     * Option to allow for the SHA256 of the host public key to be checked\n     * for validation purposes.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOSTKEYFUNCTION:\n    /* the callback to check the hostkey without the knownhost file */\n    data->set.ssh_hostkeyfunc = va_arg(param, curl_sshhostkeycallback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOSTKEYDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the SSH keyfunc callback\n     */\n    data->set.ssh_hostkeyfunc_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KEYFUNCTION:\n    /* setting to NULL is fine since the ssh.c functions themselves will\n       then revert to use the internal default */\n    data->set.ssh_keyfunc = va_arg(param, curl_sshkeycallback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KEYDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the SSH keyfunc callback\n     */\n    data->set.ssh_keyfunc_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_COMPRESSION:\n    data->set.ssh_compression = (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif /* USE_SSH */\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_TRANSFER_DECODING:\n    /*\n     * disable libcurl transfer encoding is used\n     */\n#ifndef USE_HYPER\n    data->set.http_te_skip = (0 == va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN; /* hyper doesn't support */\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_CONTENT_DECODING:\n    /*\n     * raw data passed to the application when content encoding is used\n     */\n    data->set.http_ce_skip = (0 == va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  case CURLOPT_NEW_FILE_PERMS:\n    /*\n     * Uses these permissions instead of 0644\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 0777))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.new_file_perms = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  case CURLOPT_NEW_DIRECTORY_PERMS:\n    /*\n     * Uses these permissions instead of 0755\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 0777))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.new_directory_perms = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef ENABLE_IPV6\n  case CURLOPT_ADDRESS_SCOPE:\n    /*\n     * Use this scope id when using IPv6\n     * We always get longs when passed plain numericals so we should check\n     * that the value fits into an unsigned 32 bit integer.\n     */\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n#if SIZEOF_LONG > 4\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n#endif\n    data->set.scope_id = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS:\n    /* set the bitmask for the protocols that are allowed to be used for the\n       transfer, which thus helps the app which takes URLs from users or other\n       external inputs and want to restrict what protocol(s) to deal\n       with. Defaults to CURLPROTO_ALL. */\n    data->set.allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_REDIR_PROTOCOLS:\n    /* set the bitmask for the protocols that libcurl is allowed to follow to,\n       as a subset of the CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS ones. That means the protocol needs\n       to be set in both bitmasks to be allowed to get redirected to. */\n    data->set.redir_protocols = (curl_prot_t)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS_STR: {\n    curl_prot_t prot;\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    result = protocol2num(argptr, &prot);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    data->set.allowed_protocols = prot;\n    break;\n  }\n\n  case CURLOPT_REDIR_PROTOCOLS_STR: {\n    curl_prot_t prot;\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    result = protocol2num(argptr, &prot);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    data->set.redir_protocols = prot;\n    break;\n  }\n\n  case CURLOPT_DEFAULT_PROTOCOL:\n    /* Set the protocol to use when the URL doesn't include any protocol */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DEFAULT_PROTOCOL],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_FROM:\n    /* Set the SMTP mail originator */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_MAIL_FROM],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_AUTH:\n    /* Set the SMTP auth originator */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_MAIL_AUTH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT:\n    /* Set the list of mail recipients */\n    data->set.mail_rcpt = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT_ALLOWFAILS:\n    /* allow RCPT TO command to fail for some recipients */\n    data->set.mail_rcpt_allowfails = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SASL_AUTHZID:\n    /* Authorization identity (identity to act as) */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SASL_AUTHZID],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SASL_IR:\n    /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\n    data->set.sasl_ir = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_REQUEST:\n  {\n    /*\n     * Set the RTSP request method (OPTIONS, SETUP, PLAY, etc...)\n     * Would this be better if the RTSPREQ_* were just moved into here?\n     */\n    long in_rtspreq = va_arg(param, long);\n    Curl_RtspReq rtspreq = RTSPREQ_NONE;\n    switch(in_rtspreq) {\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_OPTIONS:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_SETUP:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_SETUP;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_PLAY:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_PLAY;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_PAUSE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_PAUSE;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_RECORD:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_RECORD;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_RECEIVE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_RECEIVE;\n      break;\n    default:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_NONE;\n    }\n\n    data->set.rtspreq = rtspreq;\n    break;\n  }\n\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_SESSION_ID:\n    /*\n     * Set the RTSP Session ID manually. Useful if the application is\n     * resuming a previously established RTSP session\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_RTSP_SESSION_ID],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_STREAM_URI:\n    /*\n     * Set the Stream URI for the RTSP request. Unless the request is\n     * for generic server options, the application will need to set this.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_RTSP_STREAM_URI],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_TRANSPORT:\n    /*\n     * The content of the Transport: header for the RTSP request\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_RTSP_TRANSPORT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_CLIENT_CSEQ:\n    /*\n     * Set the CSEQ number to issue for the next RTSP request. Useful if the\n     * application is resuming a previously broken connection. The CSEQ\n     * will increment from this new number henceforth.\n     */\n    data->state.rtsp_next_client_CSeq = va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_SERVER_CSEQ:\n    /* Same as the above, but for server-initiated requests */\n    data->state.rtsp_next_server_CSeq = va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_INTERLEAVEDATA:\n    data->set.rtp_out = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INTERLEAVEFUNCTION:\n    /* Set the user defined RTP write function */\n    data->set.fwrite_rtp = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_WILDCARDMATCH:\n    data->set.wildcard_enabled = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_BGN_FUNCTION:\n    data->set.chunk_bgn = va_arg(param, curl_chunk_bgn_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_END_FUNCTION:\n    data->set.chunk_end = va_arg(param, curl_chunk_end_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FNMATCH_FUNCTION:\n    data->set.fnmatch = va_arg(param, curl_fnmatch_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_DATA:\n    data->set.wildcardptr = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FNMATCH_DATA:\n    data->set.fnmatch_data = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_USERNAME:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_USERNAME:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_TYPE:\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr && !strncasecompare(argptr, \"SRP\", strlen(\"SRP\")))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_TYPE:\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr || !strncasecompare(argptr, \"SRP\", strlen(\"SRP\")))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#endif\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_ARES\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_SERVERS:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_SERVERS],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_servers(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_SERVERS]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_INTERFACE:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_INTERFACE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_interface(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_INTERFACE]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_LOCAL_IP4:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP4],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_local_ip4(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP4]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_LOCAL_IP6:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP6],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_local_ip6(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP6]);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPALIVE:\n    data->set.tcp_keepalive = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPIDLE:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n    data->set.tcp_keepidle = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPINTVL:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n    data->set.tcp_keepintvl = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_FASTOPEN:\n#if defined(CONNECT_DATA_IDEMPOTENT) || defined(MSG_FASTOPEN) || \\\n   defined(TCP_FASTOPEN_CONNECT)\n    data->set.tcp_fastopen = (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n#else\n    result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_ENABLE_NPN:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_ENABLE_ALPN:\n    data->set.ssl_enable_alpn = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  case CURLOPT_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH:\n    data->set.abstract_unix_socket = FALSE;\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ABSTRACT_UNIX_SOCKET:\n    data->set.abstract_unix_socket = TRUE;\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_PATH_AS_IS:\n    data->set.path_as_is = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PIPEWAIT:\n    data->set.pipewait = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_WEIGHT:\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg >= 1) && (arg <= 256))\n      data->set.priority.weight = (int)arg;\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS:\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E:\n  {\n    struct Curl_easy *dep = va_arg(param, struct Curl_easy *);\n    if(!dep || GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(dep)) {\n      return Curl_data_priority_add_child(dep, data,\n                                          option == CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO:\n    data->set.connect_to = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SUPPRESS_CONNECT_HEADERS:\n    data->set.suppress_connect_headers = (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      uarg = UINT_MAX;\n    data->set.happy_eyeballs_timeout = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SHUFFLE_DNS\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_SHUFFLE_ADDRESSES:\n    data->set.dns_shuffle_addresses = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_DISALLOW_USERNAME_IN_URL:\n    data->set.disallow_username_in_url =\n      (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_URL:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DOH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    data->set.doh = data->set.str[STRING_DOH]?TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_UPKEEP_INTERVAL_MS:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.upkeep_interval_ms = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXAGE_CONN:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxage_conn = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXLIFETIME_CONN:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxlifetime_conn = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TRAILERFUNCTION:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    data->set.trailer_callback = va_arg(param, curl_trailer_callback);\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TRAILERDATA:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    data->set.trailer_data = va_arg(param, void *);\n#endif\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSREADFUNCTION:\n    data->set.hsts_read = va_arg(param, curl_hstsread_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSREADDATA:\n    data->set.hsts_read_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSWRITEFUNCTION:\n    data->set.hsts_write = va_arg(param, curl_hstswrite_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSWRITEDATA:\n    data->set.hsts_write_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTS: {\n    struct curl_slist *h;\n    if(!data->hsts) {\n      data->hsts = Curl_hsts_init();\n      if(!data->hsts)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_HSTS], argptr);\n      if(result)\n        return result;\n      /* this needs to build a list of file names to read from, so that it can\n         read them later, as we might get a shared HSTS handle to load them\n         into */\n      h = curl_slist_append(data->set.hstslist, argptr);\n      if(!h) {\n        curl_slist_free_all(data->set.hstslist);\n        data->set.hstslist = NULL;\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n      data->set.hstslist = h; /* store the list for later use */\n    }\n    else {\n      /* clear the list of HSTS files */\n      curl_slist_free_all(data->set.hstslist);\n      data->set.hstslist = NULL;\n      if(!data->share || !data->share->hsts)\n        /* throw away the HSTS cache unless shared */\n        Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_HSTS_CTRL:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg & CURLHSTS_ENABLE) {\n      if(!data->hsts) {\n        data->hsts = Curl_hsts_init();\n        if(!data->hsts)\n          return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n    }\n    else\n      Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  case CURLOPT_ALTSVC:\n    if(!data->asi) {\n      data->asi = Curl_altsvc_init();\n      if(!data->asi)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_ALTSVC], argptr);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    if(argptr)\n      (void)Curl_altsvc_load(data->asi, argptr);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ALTSVC_CTRL:\n    if(!data->asi) {\n      data->asi = Curl_altsvc_init();\n      if(!data->asi)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    result = Curl_altsvc_ctrl(data->asi, arg);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_PREREQFUNCTION:\n    data->set.fprereq = va_arg(param, curl_prereq_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PREREQDATA:\n    data->set.prereq_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n  case CURLOPT_WS_OPTIONS: {\n    bool raw;\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    raw = (arg & CURLWS_RAW_MODE);\n    data->set.ws_raw_mode = raw;\n    break;\n  }\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:\n    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;\n    break;\n  default:\n    /* unknown tag and its companion, just ignore: */\n    result = CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n    break;\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/*\n * curl_easy_setopt() is the external interface for setting options on an\n * easy handle.\n *\n * NOTE: This is one of few API functions that are allowed to be called from\n * within a callback.\n */\n\n#undef curl_easy_setopt\nCURLcode curl_easy_setopt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption tag, ...)\n{\n  va_list arg;\n  CURLcode result;\n\n  if(!data)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  va_start(arg, tag);\n\n  result = Curl_vsetopt(data, tag, arg);\n\n  va_end(arg);\n  return result;\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:",
        "     data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;",
        "     break;",
        "+#ifdef USE_HTTP3",
        "+  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {",
        "+    /* RFC 9218 Extensible Priorities for HTTP/3",
        "+       Format: \"u=<urgency>[,i]\"",
        "+       where urgency is 0-7 (default 3) and i is optional incremental flag */",
        "+    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, const char *);",
        "+    if(priority_str) {",
        "+      unsigned char urgency = 3; /* default urgency */",
        "+      bool incremental = FALSE;",
        "+",
        "+      /* Parse urgency: look for \"u=X\" where X is 0-7 */",
        "+      if(priority_str[0] == 'u' && priority_str[1] == '=') {",
        "+        char urg_char = priority_str[2];",
        "+        if(urg_char >= '0' && urg_char <= '7') {",
        "+          urgency = (unsigned char)(urg_char - '0');",
        "+",
        "+          /* Check for optional incremental flag: \",i\" */",
        "+          if(priority_str[3] == ',' && priority_str[4] == 'i') {",
        "+            incremental = TRUE;",
        "+          }",
        "+        }",
        "+      }",
        "+",
        "+#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)",
        "+      data->set.priority.urgency = urgency;",
        "+      data->set.priority.incremental = incremental ? 1 : 0;",
        "+#endif",
        "+    }",
        "+    break;",
        "+  }",
        "+#endif",
        "   default:",
        "     /* unknown tag and its companion, just ignore: */",
        "     result = CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;"
      ],
      "newLines": 37,
      "newStart": 3170,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 3170
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

51. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/url.c",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 477
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "CURLcode Curl_init_userdefined(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *set = &data->set;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  set->out = stdout; /* default output to stdout */\n  set->in_set = stdin;  /* default input from stdin */\n  set->err  = stderr;  /* default stderr to stderr */\n\n  /* use fwrite as default function to store output */\n  set->fwrite_func = (curl_write_callback)fwrite;\n\n  /* use fread as default function to read input */\n  set->fread_func_set = (curl_read_callback)fread;\n  set->is_fread_set = 0;\n\n  set->seek_func = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->seek_client = ZERO_NULL;\n\n  set->filesize = -1;        /* we don't know the size */\n  set->postfieldsize = -1;   /* unknown size */\n  set->maxredirs = 30;       /* sensible default */\n\n  set->method = HTTPREQ_GET; /* Default HTTP request */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  set->rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS; /* Default RTSP request */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->ftp_use_epsv = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPSV operations */\n  set->ftp_use_eprt = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPRT operations */\n  set->ftp_use_pret = FALSE;  /* mainly useful for drftpd servers */\n  set->ftp_filemethod = FTPFILE_MULTICWD;\n  set->ftp_skip_ip = TRUE;    /* skip PASV IP by default */\n#endif\n  set->dns_cache_timeout = 60; /* Timeout every 60 seconds by default */\n\n  /* Set the default size of the SSL session ID cache */\n  set->general_ssl.max_ssl_sessions = 5;\n  /* Timeout every 24 hours by default */\n  set->general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = 24 * 60 * 60;\n\n  set->httpauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC;  /* defaults to basic */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  set->proxyport = 0;\n  set->proxytype = CURLPROXY_HTTP; /* defaults to HTTP proxy */\n  set->proxyauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC; /* defaults to basic */\n  /* SOCKS5 proxy auth defaults to username/password + GSS-API */\n  set->socks5auth = CURLAUTH_BASIC | CURLAUTH_GSSAPI;\n#endif\n\n  /* make libcurl quiet by default: */\n  set->hide_progress = TRUE;  /* CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS changes these */\n\n  Curl_mime_initpart(&set->mimepost);\n\n  /*\n   * libcurl 7.10 introduced SSL verification *by default*! This needs to be\n   * switched off unless wanted.\n   */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  set->doh_verifyhost = TRUE;\n  set->doh_verifypeer = TRUE;\n#endif\n  set->ssl.primary.verifypeer = TRUE;\n  set->ssl.primary.verifyhost = TRUE;\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  /* defaults to any auth type */\n  set->ssh_auth_types = CURLSSH_AUTH_DEFAULT;\n  set->new_directory_perms = 0755; /* Default permissions */\n#endif\n  set->ssl.primary.sessionid = TRUE; /* session ID caching enabled by\n                                        default */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  set->proxy_ssl = set->ssl;\n#endif\n\n  set->new_file_perms = 0644;    /* Default permissions */\n  set->allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t) CURLPROTO_ALL;\n  set->redir_protocols = CURLPROTO_HTTP | CURLPROTO_HTTPS | CURLPROTO_FTP |\n                         CURLPROTO_FTPS;\n\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  /*\n   * disallow unprotected protection negotiation NEC reference implementation\n   * seem not to follow rfc1961 section 4.3/4.4\n   */\n  set->socks5_gssapi_nec = FALSE;\n#endif\n\n  /* Set the default CA cert bundle/path detected/specified at build time.\n   *\n   * If Schannel is the selected SSL backend then these locations are\n   * ignored. We allow setting CA location for schannel only when explicitly\n   * specified by the user via CURLOPT_CAINFO / --cacert.\n   */\n  if(Curl_ssl_backend() != CURLSSLBACKEND_SCHANNEL) {\n#if defined(CURL_CA_BUNDLE)\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&set->str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE], CURL_CA_BUNDLE);\n    if(result)",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/url.c",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 477,
    "totalLines": 4084
  },
  "type": "text"
}
52. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -n \"priority\" /workspace/lib/url.c",
  "description": "Check for priority in url.c"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "133:static void data_priority_cleanup(struct Curl_easy *data);\n135:#define data_priority_cleanup(x)\n427:  data_priority_cleanup(data);\n624:  memset(&set->priority, 0, sizeof(set->priority));\n3990:static void priority_remove_child(struct Curl_easy *parent,\n3993:  struct Curl_data_prio_node **pnext = &parent->set.priority.children;\n3994:  struct Curl_data_prio_node *pnode = parent->set.priority.children;\n3996:  DEBUGASSERT(child->set.priority.parent == parent);\n4008:  child->set.priority.parent = 0;\n4009:  child->set.priority.exclusive = FALSE;\n4012:CURLcode Curl_data_priority_add_child(struct Curl_easy *parent,\n4016:  if(child->set.priority.parent) {\n4017:    priority_remove_child(child->set.priority.parent, child);\n4029:    if(parent->set.priority.children && exclusive) {\n4031:      struct Curl_data_prio_node *node = parent->set.priority.children;\n4033:        node->data->set.priority.parent = child;\n4037:      tail = &child->set.priority.children;\n4042:      *tail = parent->set.priority.children;\n4043:      parent->set.priority.children = 0;\n4046:    tail = &parent->set.priority.children;\n4048:      (*tail)->data->set.priority.exclusive = FALSE;\n4056:  child->set.priority.parent = parent;\n4057:  child->set.priority.exclusive = exclusive;\n4064:static void data_priority_cleanup(struct Curl_easy *data)\n4066:  while(data->set.priority.children) {\n4067:    struct Curl_easy *tmp = data->set.priority.children->data;\n4068:    priority_remove_child(data, tmp);\n4069:    if(data->set.priority.parent)\n4070:      Curl_data_priority_add_child(data->set.priority.parent, tmp, FALSE);\n4073:  if(data->set.priority.parent)\n4074:    priority_remove_child(data->set.priority.parent, data);\n4078:void Curl_data_priority_clear_state(struct Curl_easy *data)\n4080:  memset(&data->state.priority, 0, sizeof(data->state.priority));"
}

54. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/url.c",
  "new_string": "#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  memset(&set->priority, 0, sizeof(set->priority));\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  /* RFC 9218: default urgency is 3 */\n  set->priority.urgency = 3;\n#endif\n#endif",
  "old_string": "#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  memset(&set->priority, 0, sizeof(set->priority));\n#endif",

}

*data);\n#else\n#define data_priority_cleanup(x)\n#endif\n\n/* Some parts of the code (e.g. chunked encoding) assume this buffer has at\n * more than just a few bytes to play with. Don't let it become too small or\n * bad things will happen.\n */\n#if READBUFFER_SIZE < READBUFFER_MIN\n# error READBUFFER_SIZE is too small\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n#define UNIX_SOCKET_PREFIX \"localhost\"\n#endif\n\n/* Reject URLs exceeding this length */\n#define MAX_URL_LEN 0xffff\n\n/*\n* get_protocol_family()\n*\n* This is used to return the protocol family for a given protocol.\n*\n* Parameters:\n*\n* 'h'  [in]  - struct Curl_handler pointer.\n*\n* Returns the family as a single bit protocol identifier.\n*/\nstatic curl_prot_t get_protocol_family(const struct Curl_handler *h)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(h);\n  DEBUGASSERT(h->family);\n  return h->family;\n}\n\n\n/*\n * Protocol table. Schemes (roughly) in 2019 popularity order:\n *\n * HTTPS, HTTP, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, FILE, SCP, SMTP, LDAP, IMAPS, TELNET, IMAP,\n * LDAPS, SMTPS, TFTP, SMB, POP3, GOPHER POP3S, RTSP, RTMP, SMBS, DICT\n */\nstatic const struct Curl_handler * const protocols[] = {\n\n#if defined(USE_SSL) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP)\n  &Curl_handler_https,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  &Curl_handler_http,\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n#if defined(USE_SSL) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP)\n  &Curl_handler_wss,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  &Curl_handler_ws,\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  &Curl_handler_ftp,\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_SSL) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP)\n  &Curl_handler_ftps,\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_SSH)\n  &Curl_handler_sftp,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FILE\n  &Curl_handler_file,\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_SSH) && !defined(USE_WOLFSSH)\n  &Curl_handler_scp,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  &Curl_handler_smtp,\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  &Curl_handler_smtps,\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_LDAP\n  &Curl_handler_ldap,\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_LDAPS) && \\\n &Curl_handler_imap,\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  &Curl_handler_imaps,\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TELNET\n  &Curl_handler_telnet,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n  &Curl_handler_tftp,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_POP3\n  &Curl_handler_pop3,\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  &Curl_handler_pop3s,\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMB) && defined(USE_CURL_NTLM_CORE) && \\\n   (SIZEOF_CURL_OFF_T > 4)\n  &Curl_handler_smb,\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  &Curl_handler_smbs,\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  &Curl_handler_rtsp,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MQTT\n  &Curl_handler_mqtt,\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_GOPHER\n  &Curl_handler_gopher,\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  &Curl_handler_gophers,\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_LIBRTMP\n &Curl_handler_dict,\n#endif\n\n  (struct Curl_handler *) NULL\n};\n\nvoid Curl_freeset(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* Free all dynamic strings stored in the data->set substructure. */\n  enum dupstring i;\n  enum dupblob j;\n\n  for(i = (enum dupstring)0; i < STRING_LAST; i++) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->set.str[i]);\n  }\n\n  for(j = (enum dupblob)0; j < BLOB_LAST; j++) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->set.blobs[j]);\n  }\n\n  if(data->state.referer_alloc) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.referer);\n    data->state.referer_alloc = FALSE;\n  }\n  data->state.referer = NULL;\n  if(data->state.url_alloc) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.url);\n    data->state.url_alloc = FALSE;\n  }\n  data->state.url = NULL;\n\n  Curl_mime_cleanpart(&data->set.mimepost);\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  curl_slist_free_all(data->set.cookielist);\n  data->set.cookielist = NULL;\n#endif\n}\n\n/* free the URL pieces */\nstatic void up_free(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct urlpieces *up = &data->state.up;\n  Curl_safefree(up->scheme);\n  Curl_safefree(up->hostname);\n  Curl_safefree(up->port);\n  Curl_safefree(up->user);\n  Curl_safefree(up->password);\n  Curl_safefree(up->options);\n  Curl_safefree(up->path);\n  Curl_safefree(up->query);\n  curl_url_cleanup(data->state.uh);\n  data->state.uh = NULL;\n}\n\n/*\n * This is the internal function curl_easy_cleanup() calls. This should\n * cleanup and free all resources associated with this sessionhandle.\n *\n * We ignore SIGPIPE when this is called from curl_easy_cleanup.\n */\n\nCURLcode Curl_close(struct Curl_easy **datap)\n{\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n\n  if(!datap || !*datap)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  data = *datap;\n  *datap = NULL;\n\n  Curl_expire_clear(data); /* shut off timers */\n\n  /* Detach connection if any is left. This should not be normal, but can be\n     the case for example with CONNECT_ONLY + recv/send (test 556) */\n  Curl_detach_connection(data);\n  if(data->multi)\n    /* This handle is still part of a multi handle, take care of this first\n       and detach this handle from there. */\n    curl_multi_remove_handle(data->multi, data);\n\n  if(data->multi_easy) {\n    /* when curl_easy_perform() is used, it creates its own multi handle to\n       use and this is the one */\n    curl_multi_cleanup(data->multi_easy);\n    data->multi_easy = NULL;\n  }\n\n  data->magic = 0; /* force a clear AFTER the possibly enforced removal from\n                      the multi handle, since that function uses the magic\n                      field! */\n\n  if(data->state.rangestringalloc)\n    free(data->state.range);\n\n  /* freed here just in case DONE wasn't called */\n  Curl_free_request_state(data);\n\n  /* Close down all open SSL info and sessions */\n  Curl_ssl_close_all(data);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.first_host);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.scratch);\n  Curl_ssl_free_certinfo(data);\n\n  /* Cleanup possible redirect junk */\n  free(data->req.newurl);\n  data->req.newurl = NULL;\n\n  if(data->state.referer_alloc) {\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.referer);\n    data->state.referer_alloc = FALSE;\n  }\n  data->state.referer = NULL;\n\n  up_free(data);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.buffer);\n  Curl_dyn_free(&data->state.headerb);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf);\n  Curl_flush_cookies(data, TRUE);\n  Curl_altsvc_save(data, data->asi, data->set.str[STRING_ALTSVC]);\n  Curl_altsvc_cleanup(&data->asi);\n  Curl_hsts_save(data, data->hsts, data->set.str[STRING_HSTS]);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  if(!data->share || !data->share->hsts)\n    Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n  curl_slist_free_all(data->set.hstslist); /* clean up list */\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH)\n  Curl_http_auth_cleanup_digest(data);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(data->info.contenttype);\n  Curl_safefree(data->info.wouldredirect);\n\n  /* this destroys the channel and we cannot use it anymore after this */\n  Curl_resolver_cancel(data);\n  Curl_resolver_cleanup(data->state.async.resolver);\n\n  data_priority_cleanup(data);\n\n  /* No longer a dirty share, if it exists */\n  if(data->share) {\n    Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n    data->share->dirty--;\n    Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n  }\n\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.proxyuserpwd);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.uagent);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.userpwd);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.accept_encoding);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.te);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.rangeline);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.ref);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.host);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.cookiehost);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.rtsp_transport);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.user);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.proxyuser);\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.proxypasswd);\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  if(data->req.doh) {\n    Curl_dyn_free(&data->req.doh->probe[0].serverdoh);\n    Curl_dyn_free(&data->req.doh->probe[1].serverdoh);\n    curl_slist_free_all(data->req.doh->headers);\n    Curl_safefree(data->req.doh);\n  }\n#endif\n\n  Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->state.formp);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.formp);\n#endif\n\n  /* destruct wildcard structures if it is needed */\n  Curl_wildcard_dtor(&data->wildcard);\n  Curl_freeset(data);\n  Curl_headers_cleanup(data);\n  free(data);\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * Initialize the UserDefined fields within a Curl_easy.\n * This may be safely called on a new or existing Curl_easy.\n */\nCURLcode Curl_init_userdefined(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *set = &data->set;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  set->out = stdout; /* default output to stdout */\n  set->in_set = stdin;  /* default input from stdin */\n  set->err  = stderr;  /* default stderr to stderr */\n\n  /* use fwrite as default function to store output */\n  set->fwrite_func = (curl_write_callback)fwrite;\n\n  /* use fread as default function to read input */\n  set->fread_func_set = (curl_read_callback)fread;\n  set->is_fread_set = 0;\n\n  set->seek_func = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->seek_client = ZERO_NULL;\n\n  set->filesize = -1;        /* we don't know the size */\n  set->postfieldsize = -1;   /* unknown size */\n  set->maxredirs = 30;       /* sensible default */\n\n  set->method = HTTPREQ_GET; /* Default HTTP request */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  set->rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS; /* Default RTSP request */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->ftp_use_epsv = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPSV operations */\n  set->ftp_use_eprt = TRUE;   /* FTP defaults to EPRT operations */\n  set->ftp_use_pret = FALSE;  /* mainly useful for drftpd servers */\n  set->ftp_filemethod = FTPFILE_MULTICWD;\n  set->ftp_skip_ip = TRUE;    /* skip PASV IP by default */\n#endif\n  set->dns_cache_timeout = 60; /* Timeout every 60 seconds by default */\n\n  /* Set the default size of the SSL session ID cache */\n  set->general_ssl.max_ssl_sessions = 5;\n  /* Timeout every 24 hours by default */\n  set->general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = 24 * 60 * 60;\n\n  set->httpauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC;  /* defaults to basic */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  set->proxyport = 0;\n  set->proxytype = CURLPROXY_HTTP; /* defaults to HTTP proxy */\n  set->proxyauth = CURLAUTH_BASIC; /* defaults to basic */\n  /* SOCKS5 proxy auth defaults to username/password + GSS-API */\n  set->socks5auth = CURLAUTH_BASIC | CURLAUTH_GSSAPI;\n#endif\n\n  /* make libcurl quiet by default: */\n  set->hide_progress = TRUE;  /* CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS changes these */\n\n  Curl_mime_initpart(&set->mimepost);\n\n  /*\n   * libcurl 7.10 introduced SSL verification *by default*! This needs to be\n   * switched off unless wanted.\n   */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  set->doh_verifyhost = TRUE;\n  set->doh_verifypeer = TRUE;\n#endif\n  set->ssl.primary.verifypeer = TRUE;\n  set->ssl.primary.verifyhost = TRUE;\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  /* defaults to any auth type */\n  set->ssh_auth_types = CURLSSH_AUTH_DEFAULT;\n  set->new_directory_perms = 0755; /* Default permissions */\n#endif\n  set->ssl.primary.sessionid = TRUE; /* session ID caching enabled by\n                                        default */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  set->proxy_ssl = set->ssl;\n#endif\n\n  set->new_file_perms = 0644;    /* Default permissions */\n  set->allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t) CURLPROTO_ALL;\n  set->redir_protocols = CURLPROTO_HTTP | CURLPROTO_HTTPS | CURLPROTO_FTP |\n                         CURLPROTO_FTPS;\n\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  /*\n   * disallow unprotected protection negotiation NEC reference implementation\n   * seem not to follow rfc1961 section 4.3/4.4\n   */\n  set->socks5_gssapi_nec = FALSE;\n#endif\n\n  /* Set the default CA cert bundle/path detected/specified at build time.\n   *\n   * If Schannel is the selected SSL backend then these locations are\n   * ignored. We allow setting CA location for schannel only when explicitly\n   * specified by the user via CURLOPT_CAINFO / --cacert.\n   */\n  if(Curl_ssl_backend() != CURLSSLBACKEND_SCHANNEL) {\n#if defined(CURL_CA_BUNDLE)\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&set->str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE], CURL_CA_BUNDLE);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&set->str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE_PROXY],\n                            CURL_CA_BUNDLE);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n#endif\n#if defined(CURL_CA_PATH)\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&set->str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH], CURL_CA_PATH);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&set->str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH_PROXY], CURL_CA_PATH);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n#endif\n  }\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  set->wildcard_enabled = FALSE;\n  set->chunk_bgn      = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->chunk_end      = ZERO_NULL;\n  set->fnmatch = ZERO_NULL;\n#endif\n  set->tcp_keepalive = FALSE;\n  set->tcp_keepintvl = 60;\n  set->tcp_keepidle = 60;\n  set->tcp_fastopen = FALSE;\n  set->tcp_nodelay = TRUE;\n  set->ssl_enable_alpn = TRUE;\n  set->expect_100_timeout = 1000L; /* Wait for a second by default. */\n  set->sep_headers = TRUE; /* separated header lists by default */\n  set->buffer_size = READBUFFER_SIZE;\n  set->upload_buffer_size = UPLOADBUFFER_DEFAULT;\n  set->happy_eyeballs_timeout = CURL_HET_DEFAULT;\n  set->upkeep_interval_ms = CURL_UPKEEP_INTERVAL_DEFAULT;\n  set->maxconnects = DEFAULT_CONNCACHE_SIZE; /* for easy handles */\n  set->maxage_conn = 118;\n  set->maxlifetime_conn = 0;\n  set->http09_allowed = FALSE;\n#ifdef USE_HTTP2\n  set->httpwant = CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS\n#else\n  set->httpwant = CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1\n#endif\n    ;\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  memset(&set->priority, 0, sizeof(set->priority));\n#endif\n  set->quick_exit = 0L;\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Curl_open()\n *\n * @param curl is a pointer to a sessionhandle pointer that gets set by this\n * function.\n * @return CURLcode\n */\n\nCURLcode Curl_open(struct Curl_easy **curl)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n\n  /* Very simple start-up: alloc the struct, init it with zeroes and return */\n  data = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_easy));\n  if(!data) {\n    /* this is a very serious error */\n    DEBUGF(fprintf(stderr, \"Error: calloc of Curl_easy failed\\n\"));\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  data->magic = CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER;\n\n  result = Curl_resolver_init(data, &data->state.async.resolver);\n  if(result) {\n    DEBUGF(fprintf(stderr, \"Error: resolver_init failed\\n\"));\n    free(data);\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  result = Curl_init_userdefined(data);\n  if(!result) {\n    Curl_dyn_init(&data->state.headerb, CURL_MAX_HTTP_HEADER);\n    Curl_initinfo(data);\n\n    /* most recent connection is not yet defined */\n    data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\n    data->state.recent_conn_id = -1;\n    /* and not assigned an id yet */\n    data->id = -1;\n\n    data->progress.flags |= PGRS_HIDE;\n    data->state.current_speed = -1; /* init to negative == impossible */\n  }\n\n  if(result) {\n    Curl_resolver_cleanup(data->state.async.resolver);\n    Curl_dyn_free(&data->state.headerb);\n    Curl_freeset(data);\n    free(data);\n    data = NULL;\n  }\n  else\n    *curl = data;\n\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic void conn_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n  infof(data, \"Closing connection\");\n\n  /* possible left-overs from the async name resolvers */\n  Curl_resolver_cancel(data);\n\n  Curl_conn_close(data, SECONDARYSOCKET);\n  Curl_conn_close(data, FIRSTSOCKET);\n}\n\nstatic void conn_free(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n  size_t i;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n\n  for(i = 0; i < ARRAYSIZE(conn->cfilter); ++i) {\n    Curl_conn_cf_discard_all(data, conn, (int)i);\n  }\n\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->host);\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->conn_to_host);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->http_proxy.host);\n  Curl_free_idnconverted_hostname(&conn->socks_proxy.host);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->http_proxy.user);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->socks_proxy.user);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->http_proxy.passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->socks_proxy.passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->http_proxy.host.rawalloc); /* http proxy name buffer */\n  Curl_safefree(conn->socks_proxy.host.rawalloc); /* socks proxy name buffer */\n  Curl_free_primary_ssl_config(&conn->proxy_ssl_config);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(conn->user);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->passwd);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->sasl_authzid);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->options);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->oauth_bearer);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  Curl_dyn_free(&conn->trailer);\n#endif\n  Curl_safefree(conn->host.rawalloc); /* host name buffer */\n  Curl_safefree(conn->conn_to_host.rawalloc); /* host name buffer */\n  Curl_safefree(conn->hostname_resolve);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->secondaryhostname);\n  Curl_safefree(conn->localdev);\n  Curl_free_primary_ssl_config(&conn->ssl_config);\n\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  Curl_safefree(conn->unix_domain_socket);\n#endif\n\n  free(conn); /* free all the connection oriented data */\n}\n\n/*\n * Disconnects the given connection. Note the connection may not be the\n * primary connection, like when freeing room in the connection cache or\n * killing of a dead old connection.\n *\n * A connection needs an easy handle when closing down. We support this passed\n * in separately since the connection to get closed here is often already\n * disassociated from an easy handle.\n *\n * This function MUST NOT reset state in the Curl_easy struct if that\n * isn't strictly bound to the life-time of *this* particular connection.\n *\n */\n\nvoid Curl_disconnect(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                     struct connectdata *conn, bool dead_connection)\n{\n  /* there must be a connection to close */\n  DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n\n  /* it must be removed from the connection cache */\n  DEBUGASSERT(!conn->bundle);\n\n  /* there must be an associated transfer */\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n\n  /* the transfer must be detached from the connection */\n  DEBUGASSERT(!data->conn);\n\n  DEBUGF(infof(data, \"Curl_disconnect(conn #%\"\n         CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \", dead=%d)\",\n         conn->connection_id, dead_connection));\n  /*\n   * If this connection isn't marked to force-close, leave it open if there\n   * are other users of it\n   */\n  if(CONN_INUSE(conn) && !dead_connection) {\n    DEBUGF(infof(data, \"Curl_disconnect when inuse: %zu\", CONN_INUSE(conn)));\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if(conn->dns_entry) {\n    Curl_resolv_unlock(data, conn->dns_entry);\n    conn->dns_entry = NULL;\n  }\n\n  /* Cleanup NTLM connection-related data */\n  Curl_http_auth_cleanup_ntlm(conn);\n\n  /* Cleanup NEGOTIATE connection-related data */\n  Curl_http_auth_cleanup_negotiate(conn);\n\n  if(conn->connect_only)\n    /* treat the connection as dead in CONNECT_ONLY situations */\n    dead_connection = TRUE;\n\n  /* temporarily attach the connection to this transfer handle for the\n     disconnect and shutdown */\n  Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n\n  if(conn->handler && conn->handler->disconnect)\n    /* This is set if protocol-specific cleanups should be made */\n    conn->handler->disconnect(data, conn, dead_connection);\n\n  conn_shutdown(data);\n\n  /* detach it again */\n  Curl_detach_connection(data);\n\n  conn_free(data, conn);\n}\n\n/*\n * IsMultiplexingPossible()\n *\n * Return a bitmask with the available multiplexing options for the given\n * requested connection.\n */\nstatic int IsMultiplexingPossible(const struct Curl_easy *handle,\n                                  const struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n  int avail = 0;\n\n  /* If an HTTP protocol and multiplexing is enabled */\n  if((conn->handler->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP) &&\n     (!conn->bits.protoconnstart || !conn->bits.close)) {\n\n    if(Curl_multiplex_wanted(handle->multi) &&\n       (handle->state.httpwant >= CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2))\n      /* allows HTTP/2 */\n      avail |= CURLPIPE_MULTIPLEX;\n  }\n  return avail;\n}\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\nstatic bool\nproxy_info_matches(const struct proxy_info *data,\n                   const struct proxy_info *needle)\n{\n  if((data->proxytype == needle->proxytype) &&\n     (data->port == needle->port) &&\n     strcasecompare(data->host.name, needle->host.name))\n    return TRUE;\n\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\nstatic bool\nsocks_proxy_info_matches(const struct proxy_info *data,\n                         const struct proxy_info *needle)\n{\n  if(!proxy_info_matches(data, needle))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  /* the user information is case-sensitive\n     or at least it is not defined as case-insensitive\n     see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3986#section-3.2.1 */\n\n  /* curl_strequal does a case insensitive comparison,\n     so do not use it here! */\n  if(Curl_timestrcmp(data->user, needle->user) ||\n     Curl_timestrcmp(data->passwd, needle->passwd))\n    return FALSE;\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#else\n/* disabled, won't get called */\n#define proxy_info_matches(x,y) FALSE\n#define socks_proxy_info_matches(x,y) FALSE\n#endif\n\n/* A connection has to have been idle for a shorter time than 'maxage_conn'\n   (the success rate is just too low after this), or created less than\n   'maxlifetime_conn' ago, to be subject for reuse. */\n\nstatic bool conn_maxage(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                        struct connectdata *conn,\n                        struct curltime now)\n{\n  timediff_t idletime, lifetime;\n\n  idletime = Curl_timediff(now, conn->lastused);\n  idletime /= 1000; /* integer seconds is fine */\n\n  if(idletime > data->set.maxage_conn) {\n    infof(data, \"Too old connection (%\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n          \" seconds idle), disconnect it\", idletime);\n    return TRUE;\n  }\n\n  lifetime = Curl_timediff(now, conn->created);\n  lifetime /= 1000; /* integer seconds is fine */\n\n  if(data->set.maxlifetime_conn && lifetime > data->set.maxlifetime_conn) {\n    infof(data,\n          \"Too old connection (%\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n          \" seconds since creation), disconnect it\", lifetime);\n    return TRUE;\n  }\n\n\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n/*\n * This function checks if the given connection is dead and extracts it from\n * the connection cache if so.\n *\n * When this is called as a Curl_conncache_foreach() callback, the connection\n * cache lock is held!\n *\n * Returns TRUE if the connection was dead and extracted.\n */\nstatic bool extract_if_dead(struct connectdata *conn,\n                            struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  if(!CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\n    /* The check for a dead socket makes sense only if the connection isn't in\n       use */\n    bool dead;\n    struct curltime now = Curl_now();\n    if(conn_maxage(data, conn, now)) {\n      /* avoid check if already too old */\n      dead = TRUE;\n    }\n    else if(conn->handler->connection_check) {\n      /* The protocol has a special method for checking the state of the\n         connection. Use it to check if the connection is dead. */\n      unsigned int state;\n\n      /* briefly attach the connection to this transfer for the purpose of\n         checking it */\n      Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n\n      state = conn->handler->connection_check(data, conn, CONNCHECK_ISDEAD);\n      dead = (state & CONNRESULT_DEAD);\n      /* detach the connection again */\n      Curl_detach_connection(data);\n\n    }\n    else {\n      bool input_pending;\n\n      Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n      dead = !Curl_conn_is_alive(data, conn, &input_pending);\n      if(input_pending) {\n        /* For reuse, we want a \"clean\" connection state. The includes\n         * that we expect - in general - no waiting input data. Input\n         * waiting might be a TLS Notify Close, for example. We reject\n         * that.\n         * For protocols where data from other end may arrive at\n         * any time (HTTP/2 PING for example), the protocol handler needs\n         * to install its own `connection_check` callback.\n         */\n        dead = TRUE;\n      }\n      Curl_detach_connection(data);\n    }\n\n    if(dead) {\n      infof(data, \"Connection %\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \" seems to be dead\",\n            conn->connection_id);\n      Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, conn, FALSE);\n      return TRUE;\n    }\n  }\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\nstruct prunedead {\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n  struct connectdata *extracted;\n};\n\n/*\n * Wrapper to use extract_if_dead() function in Curl_conncache_foreach()\n *\n */\nstatic int call_extract_if_dead(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                struct connectdata *conn, void *param)\n{\n  struct prunedead *p = (struct prunedead *)param;\n  if(extract_if_dead(conn, data)) {\n    /* stop the iteration here, pass back the connection that was extracted */\n    p->extracted = conn;\n    return 1;\n  }\n  return 0; /* continue iteration */\n}\n\n/*\n * This function scans the connection cache for half-open/dead connections,\n * closes and removes them.
